query: Ilya Sutskever NeurIPS Test of Time Award consecutive National Academy Sciences Award 2026

{"query":"Ilya Sutskever NeurIPS Test of Time Award consecutive National Academy Sciences Award 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1560,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://blog.neurips.cc/tag/awards/","title":"awards – NeurIPS Blog","content":"Dec 11, 2023 — Ilya Sutskever, Kai Chen, Greg Corrado, and Jeffrey Dean, won. cited over 40,000 times, 3:05 – 3:25 pm CST. NeurIPS 2026 Position Paper Track ...
Communications Chairs 2023 2023 Conference awards, neurips2023
We are honored to announce the award-winning papers for NeurIPS 2023!
This year’s organizers received a record number of paper submissions.
Of the 13,300 submitted papers that were reviewed by 968 Area Chairs, 98 senior area chairs, and 396 Ethics reviewers 3,540 were accepted after 502 papers were flagged for ethics reviews.
However, this hybrid ML-physics simulation approach requires domain-specific treatment and has been inaccessible to ML experts because of lack of training data and relevant, easy-to-use workflows.
To this end, this work proposes a comprehensive trustworthiness evaluation for large language models with a focus on GPT-4 and GPT-3.5, considering diverse perspectives – including toxicity, stereotype bias, adversarial robustness, out-of-distribution robustness, robustness on adversarial demonstrations, privacy, machine ethics, and fairness.
We also find that although GPT-4 is usually more trustworthy than GPT-3.5 on standard benchmarks, GPT-4 is more vulnerable given jailbreaking system or user prompts, potentially due to the reason that GPT-4 follows the (misleading) instructions more precisely.
This year, following the usual practice, we chose a NeurIPS paper from 10 years ago to receive the Test of Time Award, and “Distributed Representations of Words and Phrases and their Compositionality” by Tomas Mikolov, Ilya Sutskever, Kai Chen, Greg Corrado, and Jeffrey Dean, won."},{"url":"https://blog.neurips.cc/2024/11/27/announcing-the-neurips-2024-test-of-time-paper-awards/","title":"Announcing the NeurIPS 2024 Test of Time Paper Awards – NeurIPS Blog","content":"This award is intended to recognize papers published 10 years ago at NeurIPS 2014 that have significantly shaped the research field since then, standing the test of time.
This year, we are making an exception to award two Test of Time papers given the undeniable influence of these two papers on the entire field.
It is one of the foundational pieces for generative modeling and has inspired numerous research advances in the past 10 years.
Besides research, it has also enabled generative modeling to make an impact in a diverse range of applications, considering vision data and other domains.
Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks has been cited more than 27,000 times as of this blog post.
With the current fast advances of large language models and foundation models in general, making a paradigm shift in AI and applications, the field has benefited from the foundation laid by this work.
We look forward to announcing other NeurIPS awards at the conference!"}]}

","score":0.57006866,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nasonline.org/award/nas-award-for-the-industrial-application-of-science","title":"NAS Award for the Industrial Application of Science – NAS","content":"Image 1: National Academy of Sciences logo. *   Committee on Women in Science, Engineering and Medicine. *   Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences. # NAS Award for the Industrial Application of Science. Image 3: NAS Award for the Industrial Application of Science. (SSI), received the 2026 NAS Award for the Industrial Application of Science, presented for the first time in artificial intelligence. His work has redefined the boundaries of machine intelligence through research breakthroughs that include AlexNet, Sequence-to-Sequence learning, and the GPT models, and through his contributions to CLIP, DALL-E and AlphaGo. Sutskever co-founded OpenAI in 2015, and under his leadership as Chief Scientist, the company achieved the research breakthroughs that led to large language models and to the launch of ChatGPT. The NAS Award for the Industrial Application of Science was established by the IBM Corporation in honor of its executive and applied mathematician **Ralph E. Previous recipients of the NAS Award for the Industrial Application of Science continue to achieve outstanding advancements in their fields.","score":0.5393963,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://cen.acs.org/acs-news/National-Academy-Sciences-honors-scientific/104/web/2026/02","title":"National Academy of Sciences honors scientific achievement across fields with 2026 award winners","content":"Cravatt** of Scripps Research will receive the NAS Award in Chemical Sciences for his work in enzyme function and dysregulation in disease. Barch** of Washington University in St. Louis and **Tor Wager** of Dartmouth College will each receive an Atkinson Prize in Psychological and Cognitive Sciences, Barch for her work in developmental psychopathology, and Wager for his work in how the brain shapes pain, emotion, and belief. **Irit Dveer Dinur** of the Institute for Advanced Study and the Weizmann Institute of Science; **Subhash Khot** of New York University; **Guy Kindler** of the Hebrew University of Jerusalem; **Dor Minzer** of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology; and **Muli Safra** of Tel Aviv University will receive the Michael and Sheila Held Prize for their work in theoretical computer science. **James Schnable** of the University of Nebraska–Lincoln will receive the NAS Prize in Food and Agriculture Sciences for his work in plant genomics and quantitative genetics.","score":0.37717408,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://x.com/GoogleAI/status/1867338363818455052","title":"Congratulations to Ilya Sutskever, Oriol Vinyals and Quoc V ...","content":"Congratulations to Ilya Sutskever, Oriol Vinyals and Quoc V. Le for their paper “Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks", for winning the #NeurIPS","score":0.3699755,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/theNASciences/posts/ilya-sutskever-safe-superintelligence-inc-has-played-a-central-role-in-advancing/1389842603175803","title":"Ilya Sutskever (Safe... - National Academy of Sciences","content":"## National Academy of Sciences's Post. ### **National Academy of Sciences**. As one of the most highly cited computer scientists, he helped drive the deep learning revolution and expand the boundaries of machine intelligence. As a co-founder of OpenAI, Sutskever led research that enabled the development of large language models and systems like ChatGPT, and later directed efforts focused on AI safety and alignment.
